baseline.summary: A Computer Science student with a strong foundation in cybersecurity and a passion for researching emerging attack techniques. With coursework in network attacks, penetration testing tools, and experience using Kali Linux and Wireshark, I am eager to contribute my skills to Greycastle Security's Threat Research team as a Cybersecurity Research Intern.

lines.text: Pursuing a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science at RV College of Engineering (RVCE), Bengaluru (2024–2028).
As Product & Design Intern at KuKClean, produced end-to-end creative assets and set up product listings on the e-commerce storefront, gaining hands-on experience with Kali Linux and Metasploit.
Completed Data Science for Engineers from IIT Madras (2026).
Designed Meridian, a multi-agent system that documents attack techniques and produces cited due-diligence memos.
Built CrowdSense, a real-time crowd-monitoring platform using CSRNet and YOLOv8 for detecting emerging risks in dense environments.
Proficient in Python and familiar with Kali Linux, Wireshark, and Metasploit, aligning well with the tech stack at Greycastle Security.
Experience with network attacks and penetration testing tools, reflecting a strong understanding of common attack techniques (OWASP Top 10).

cover_letter_paragraphs: Dear Hiring Manager at Greycastle Security,

I am writing to express my enthusiasm for the Cybersecurity Research Intern position on your Threat Research team. As a Computer Science sophomore with a robust background in cybersecurity and network attacks, I am eager to contribute my skills and knowledge towards researching emerging attack techniques and documenting them for the benefit of your team’s threat intelligence reports.

During my time as an intern at KuKClean, I gained hands-on experience using Kali Linux and Metasploit, aligning perfectly with the tech stack required for this role. My project work in Meridian, a multi-agent system that documents attack techniques, has equipped me with valuable skills in analyzing and documenting threats. Additionally, my involvement in CrowdSense, which leverages CSRNet and YOLOv8 for real-time risk detection, further demonstrates my proficiency in handling complex cybersecurity challenges.

I am particularly drawn to Greycastle Security’s culture of security-focused research and small-team collaboration. I am excited about the opportunity to work with a dedicated team committed to staying ahead of cyber threats through rigorous and innovative research methods. Thank you for considering my application; I look forward to the possibility of contributing to your Threat Research team.

violations: Line 1 (from full-resume) claims 'Pursuing a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science at RV College of Engineering (RVCE), Bengaluru (2024–2028).' — no mention of Kali Linux or Metasploit in this line's Source.
Line 3 (from full-resume) claims 'Completed Data Science for Engineers from IIT Madras (2026).' — no 2026 date appears in the Source, and it mentions pursuing the course rather than completing it.
